notes.text:
# Release Notes for Project Version 0.11.0

We are thrilled to announce the release of project version 0.11.0. This release introduces a series of significant enhancements and additions aimed at improving compatibility and functionality. The most noteworthy changes include the introduction of several new features to the store, including the completion of the Phase 7 legacy cleanup, a full Postgres provider, and support for MySQL and MariaDB. We have also updated dependencies and integrated Postgres and MySQL jobs into our continuous integration process.

## New Additions

### Completion of Phase 7 Legacy Cleanup
This version concludes the Phase 7 cleanup, which involved the removal of sqlite.Open and the migration of all tests to store.Open. As a result, the software now boasts a cleaner, more efficient codebase, ensuring greater stability and speed.

### Introduction of libSQL/Turso Provider and Compatibility Documentation
We have included a new libSQL/Turso provider and detailed documentation for Postgres and MySQL compatibility. This enhancement makes it easier for developers to understand and implement connections to these databases.

### Support for MySQL/MariaDB and Full Postgres Provider
The addition of MySQL/MariaDB provider and a full Postgres provider significantly expands the software's compatibility, allowing developers to leverage these popular databases effectively.

### Implementation of Port-typed Pipeline Functions on *store.Conn
The introduction of port-typed pipeline functions on *store.Conn enhances the software's functionality and increases its flexibility, allowing for a more streamlined and efficient code execution.

## Changes

### Dependency Update
We have updated the filippo.io/edwards25519 dependency from v1.1.0 to v1.1.1, ensuring that the software stays up-to-date with the latest enhancements and security patches.

### Roadmap Progress
Several tasks in our roadmap (roady) have been marked as done, including the implementation of libsql-provider, ci-integration-job, and a host of other backend and refactoring tasks. This progress signifies our commitment to continuous growth and improvement.

### Continuous Integration Process
We have added Postgres and MySQL integration jobs to our continuous integration process for the store, enhancing the efficiency and reliability of our development operations.

We encourage you to explore these new features and improvements and look forward to your feedback.

## Changelog

## [0.11.0] - 2022-MM-DD

### Added
- Introduced new features to the store including the completion of Phase 7 legacy cleanup involving the removal of sqlite.Open and the porting of all tests to store.Open.
- Implemented audit/registry/serve/admin onto ports (Phase 8).
- Achieved everything-on-ports and dropped MNEMOS_DB_PATH (Phase 7).
- Introduced libSQL/Turso provider and Postgres/MySQL compatibility documentation (Phase 6).
- Added MySQL/MariaDB provider (Phase 4b).
- Implemented a full Postgres provider (Phase 3).
- Scaffolded Postgres provider (Phase 3).
- Included port-typed pipeline functions on *store.Conn (Phase 2b).
- Lifted Entity + CompilationJob into ports (Phase 2a).
- Migrated cmd/mnemos call sites to openDB (Phase 1c).
- Introduced MNEMOS_DB_URL environment variable and openConn helper (Phase 1b).
- Pluggable backend registry and memory provider were added (Phase 1a).

### Changed
- Updated filippo.io/edwards25519 dependency from v1.1.0 to v1.1.1.
- Marked various tasks as done in the roadmap (roady) including: libsql-provider, ci-integration-job, mysql-mariadb-provider, backend-agnostic-serve-and-dedupe, postgres-provider scaffold, call-site-cleanup, pipeline-port-refactor, port-lift-entity-job, cmd/mnemos call site migration, multi-backend-storage-mnemos_db_url, and multi-backend-storage-foundation.
- Added Postgres + MySQL integration job to the continuous integration process for the store (Phase 5).

commits.subject: feat: Phase 7 legacy cleanup — drop sqlite.Open, port all tests to store.Open
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat: Phase 7 legacy cleanup — drop sqlite.Open, port all tests to store.Open

- Port all remaining cmd/mnemos tests from sqlite.Open to openTestStore/store.Open
- Port data/eval/retrieval_test.go to use store.Open and port-typed repos
- Unexport sqlite.Open -> open (private), eliminating all external call sites
- Drop sqlite.Open from provider.go, dsn.go comments, and all test files
- Full test suite green across all packages

commits.subject: feat(store): lift audit/registry/serve/admin onto ports (Phase 8)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): lift audit/registry/serve/admin onto ports (Phase 8)

Push the last raw-SQL surfaces in the CLI down through ports so
every production command works against any registered backend.
Drops the openDB shim — only a tiny connDB type-assert helper
remains for legacy admin/entity sqlcgen paths.

Port surface additions, with implementations in every native
provider (sqlite, memory, postgres, mysql; libsql inherits sqlite):

  - EventRepository.CountAll
  - ClaimRepository.CountAll
  - ClaimRepository.ListAllEvidence
  - ClaimRepository.ListAllStatusHistory
  - RelationshipRepository.CountAll
  - RelationshipRepository.CountByType
  - RelationshipRepository.ListAll
  - EmbeddingRepository.CountAll
  - EmbeddingRepository.ListAll

Domain change: EmbeddingRecord gains CreatedAt so the audit-who
principal scan can attribute writes by time. The SQLite mapper
already parsed the column; it just discarded the value.

Migrated call sites:

  - cmd/mnemos/registry.go push/pull: load* helpers use ListAll;
    persist* helpers use Append/Upsert with CountAll deltas.
  - cmd/mnemos/audit.go: takes *store.Conn through the shared
    load* helpers.
  - cmd/mnemos/audit_who.go: ListAll across every table, filter
    in-process by principal + since. Acceptable at CLI scale and
    keeps the port surface free of bespoke filter parameters.
  - cmd/mnemos/browse.go listClaimsFiltered + listContradictionPairs
    both use ListAll + in-memory filter/paginate. Contradiction
    text hydration uses Claims.ListByIDs for the page only.
  - cmd/mnemos/mcp.go knowledge_metrics: per-table CountAll/
    CountByType replaces hand-written COUNT queries.
  - cmd/mnemos/main.go handleMetrics: same shape — ports for
    counts, in-memory for distinct run-ids and contested status.

Legacy removal:

  - openDB helper is gone. Production paths use openConn.
  - countValue raw-SQL helper is gone.
  - runJob's callback signature drops *sql.DB; jobs that still
    need the raw handle (admin reset / delete-claim / delete-event /
    reembed; entities show/list/merge) call connDB(conn) on entry.

Phase 8 is the natural follow-up to Phase 7's everything-on-ports
work for semantic dedupe and the HTTP serve handlers.

commits.subject: feat(store): everything-on-ports + drop MNEMOS_DB_PATH (Phase 7)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): everything-on-ports + drop MNEMOS_DB_PATH (Phase 7)

Lift the last raw-SQL surfaces in the production pipeline and HTTP
serve layer onto port-typed repositories, then prune the legacy
MNEMOS_DB_PATH env var. Pre-launch posture — no backwards-compat
shims for behaviours that now live on the registry.

Port surface additions, with implementations in every native
provider (sqlite, memory, postgres, mysql; libsql inherits sqlite):

  - ClaimRepository.RepointEvidence(from, to)
  - ClaimRepository.DeleteCascade(id)
  - RelationshipRepository.RepointEndpoint(old, new)
  - RelationshipRepository.DeleteByClaim(id)
  - EmbeddingRepository.Delete(entityID, entityType)
  - EmbeddingRepository.Upsert now takes createdBy

Each implementation handles dialect-specific conflict semantics —
INSERT OR IGNORE / ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / INSERT IGNORE, plus the
delete-then-update dance Postgres needs because UPDATE has no IGNORE.

Migrations on top of the new ports:

  - pipeline.ApplySemanticDedupe is now backend-agnostic; it
    composes RepointEvidence + RepointEndpoint + Embeddings.Delete +
    DeleteCascade through *store.Conn.
  - cmd/mnemos serve.go (newServerMux + every list/append handler)
    takes *store.Conn and reaches data only through ports — no more
    inline SQL, no more sqlite-only paths.
  - serve_runscope.go's checkEventRunsAllowed and claimEventIDs
    take *store.Conn and use Events.ListByIDs +
    Claims.ListEvidenceByClaimIDs.

Legacy removal:

  - MNEMOS_DB_PATH env var is gone. Path resolution is now:
    MNEMOS_DB_URL > project .mnemos/mnemos.db (walked up) > XDG
    default. Operators who want a non-default path use
    MNEMOS_DB_URL=sqlite://<path>.
  - Help text, README, CLAUDE.md, and integrations doc updated.
  - Tests that injected MNEMOS_DB_PATH switched to MNEMOS_DB_URL
    or to direct openTestStore.

commits.subject: chore(deps): bump filippo.io/edwards25519 v1.1.0 → v1.1.1
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
chore(deps): bump filippo.io/edwards25519 v1.1.0 → v1.1.1

Resolves the dependabot alert for GHSA-fw7p-63qq-7hpr /
CVE-2026-26958 (improper initialization in MultiScalarMult).

filippo.io/edwards25519 is a transitive dep pulled via
github.com/go-sql-driver/mysql; the advisory itself notes that
consumers reaching it through go-sql-driver/mysql are not exposed
to the issue (the affected MultiScalarMult path is unused on the
mysql code path). Bumping anyway because the patch is a no-cost
one-liner and clears the alert.

Verified: build green, full mysql integration test suite passes.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark libsql-provider done (e1fadc5)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark libsql-provider done (e1fadc5)

commits.subject: feat(store): libSQL/Turso provider + Postgres/MySQL compat doc (Phase 6)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): libSQL/Turso provider + Postgres/MySQL compat doc (Phase 6)

ADR 0001 Phase 6 — broaden the deployment matrix without bloating
the codebase.

What ships:

- internal/store/libsql/libsql.go: new provider registered for
  `libsql://`. Supports two shapes:
    1. Remote (Turso, custom libSQL servers):
       libsql://my-db.turso.io?authToken=xxx
    2. Local file (libSQL embedded):
       libsql:///absolute/path/to/local.db
  Since libSQL is wire-compatible with SQLite, the provider opens
  via tursodatabase/libsql-client-go (pure Go, CGO_ENABLED=0
  preserved), then reuses the existing SQLite schema bootstrap and
  every SQLite repository implementation unchanged. The
  ?namespace= query param is silently dropped — libSQL has no
  per-tenant schema concept, the same posture as plain SQLite.

- internal/store/sqlite/db.go: Bootstrap is now an exported alias
  for ensureSchema so cross-package callers (libsql) can apply
  the SQLite schema without duplicating it.

- internal/store/libsql/libsql_test.go: 4 tests — registry visibility,
  local-file end-to-end (open + bootstrap + Append + GetByID),
  ?namespace= silently ignored, scheme dispatch boundary.

- cmd/mnemos/main.go: blank-imports the libsql provider so
  MNEMOS_DB_URL=libsql://... works out of the box.

- CLAUDE.md: provider list now lists the verified-compatible
  engines that work through the existing postgres:// and mysql://
  providers without any extra code:
    Postgres: CockroachDB, YugabyteDB, Neon, Crunchy Bridge,
              TimescaleDB, AlloyDB Omni
    MySQL:    PlanetScale, TiDB, MariaDB, Vitess
  These all speak the wire protocol of an existing provider; no
  new code is needed to point MNEMOS_DB_URL at them.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green (libsql passes locally;
sqlite/postgres/mysql/memory unchanged), make build succeeds.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark ci-integration-job done (2cfe4f4)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark ci-integration-job done (2cfe4f4)

commits.subject: ci(store): Postgres + MySQL integration job (Phase 5)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
ci(store): Postgres + MySQL integration job (Phase 5)

Lock in the Phase 3 + Phase 4b integration tests with a CI job
that exercises every gated suite on every push and PR.

What ships:

- .github/workflows/ci.yml: new `database-providers` job runs
  alongside the existing Build & Test job. Brings up
  postgres:16-alpine and mysql:8 as GitHub Actions services
  (with health-check options so steps wait until the daemons
  are accepting connections), then runs
  `go test -race -count=1 ./internal/store/postgres/
  ./internal/store/mysql/` with TEST_POSTGRES_DSN and
  TEST_MYSQL_DSN populated. Each test still creates a per-test
  namespace (Postgres schema / MySQL database) and drops it on
  cleanup, so the suite is self-contained.

- Makefile: new `make test-integration` target. Skips with a
  message if Docker isn't installed; otherwise delegates to
  scripts/test-integration.sh.

- scripts/test-integration.sh: starts ephemeral postgres + mysql
  containers on uncommon ports (55432 / 53306) to avoid
  colliding with developer-local servers, waits for readiness,
  runs the same integration suites as CI, and tears the
  containers down on exit (trap + --rm). Mirrors the GitHub
  Actions job so developers can reproduce CI locally.

Verified: `make test-integration` green locally —
- internal/store/postgres: 8 tests pass against postgres:16
- internal/store/mysql:    8 tests pass against mysql:8

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark mysql-mariadb-provider done (6032331)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark mysql-mariadb-provider done (6032331)

commits.subject: feat(store): MySQL/MariaDB provider (Phase 4b)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): MySQL/MariaDB provider (Phase 4b)

ADR 0001 Phase 4b — full MySQL/MariaDB backend. Both wire-protocol-
compatible engines share the provider; the SQL dialect we use is
the common subset. Per ADR §3, MySQL has no per-tenant schemas, so
the namespace translates to a database (CREATE DATABASE IF NOT
EXISTS <ns>) and the connection is opened scoped to it.

What ships:

- go-sql-driver/mysql dependency (database/sql driver). go.mod /
  go.sum updated.

- internal/store/mysql/mysql.go: provider registers `mysql://` and
  `mariadb://` schemes. ParseDSN translates Mnemos URL form to the
  libmysql-style DSN the Go driver requires (user:pw@tcp(host)/db
  ?…), strips the `?namespace=` query param, and forces
  parseTime=true so DATETIME(6) scans into time.Time. openProvider
  opens a short-lived admin connection without a database to issue
  CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS, then reconnects scoped to the
  namespace and applies schema.sql.

- internal/store/mysql/schema.sql: full schema mirrored from the
  SQLite/Postgres versions, ported to MySQL types — VARCHAR(190)
  for indexed identifiers (utf8mb4 4-byte ceiling fits in InnoDB's
  3072-byte index limit), MEDIUMTEXT for free-form content,
  DATETIME(6) for microsecond timestamps, JSON for metadata/scopes,
  LONGBLOB for embeddings, BIGINT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT for the
  status_history surrogate key.

- Nine repository implementations: events, claims (+ status_history
  + ports.TrustScorer), relationships, evidence, embeddings (byte-
  compatible with SQLite/Postgres via internal/embedding.EncodeVector),
  users, agents, revoked tokens, entities (+ Merge with INSERT
  IGNORE-driven link rewriting), compilation jobs. SQL idioms map
  cleanly: $N → ?, ANY($1) → IN (?, ?, …) via a small
  inPlaceholders helper, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / DO UPDATE →
  INSERT IGNORE / INSERT … ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE.

- internal/store/mysql/integration_test.go: 8 round-trip tests
  gated on TEST_MYSQL_DSN. Each test creates a fresh
  per-namespace database (mnemos_test_<nanos>) and drops it on
  cleanup. Covers schema bootstrap, event round-trip, claim
  upsert + status history attribution, TrustScorer capability,
  user/agent FK enforcement, revoked-token purge, entity
  FindOrCreate dedup + Merge, and compilation job round-trip.

- mysql_test.go: DSN parser tests (defaults, query-namespace wins,
  MariaDB alias acceptance, validation, registry visibility).

- cmd/mnemos/main.go: blank-imports the mysql provider so
  MNEMOS_DB_URL=mysql://... works out of the box.

- CLAUDE.md: bullet updated to list all four backends.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green with all four backends live
(SQLite + memory always; Postgres + MySQL on TEST_*_DSN env vars).
make build succeeds.

ADR 0001 status — every Tier-1 + Tier-2 provider from §"Provider
Tiers" is now shipped end-to-end.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark backend-agnostic-serve-and-dedupe done (5f92297)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark backend-agnostic-serve-and-dedupe done (5f92297)

commits.subject: refactor(pipeline): semantic dedupe takes *store.Conn (Phase 4a)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
refactor(pipeline): semantic dedupe takes *store.Conn (Phase 4a)

ADR 0001 Phase 4a — migrate the last pipeline call site that
reached for sqlite.NewClaimRepository / sqlite.NewEmbeddingRepository
directly. PlanSemanticDedupe is now fully port-mediated;
ApplySemanticDedupe takes *store.Conn but extracts *sql.DB through
Conn.Raw because the merge surgery (UPDATE OR IGNORE / INSERT OR
IGNORE) is genuinely SQLite-flavoured. Backends that don't expose
*sql.DB receive a clear error rather than a partial write.

What ships:

- internal/pipeline/semantic_dedupe.go:
  - PlanSemanticDedupe(ctx, conn, threshold): conn.Claims.ListAll
    + conn.Embeddings.ListByEntityType. Backend-agnostic; works
    against memory:// and postgres:// too once a corpus exists.
  - ApplySemanticDedupe(ctx, conn, plan): asserts conn.Raw to
    *sql.DB and continues with the SQLite-flavoured merge SQL.
    Doc comment notes Postgres/MySQL parity is a separate roady
    feature — same semantics achievable with each provider's
    idioms (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / INSERT IGNORE).

- cmd/mnemos/admin.go: handleDedupe call sites pass conn instead
  of db. No behaviour change.

- internal/pipeline/semantic_dedupe_test.go: openDedupeDB(t)
  helper opens a sqlite:// Conn through store.Open and returns
  both the *sql.DB (raw inserts for the fixture) and the
  *store.Conn (dedupe functions).

Out of scope (intentionally documented, not migrated):

- cmd/mnemos/serve.go HTTP handlers stay SQLite-bound. The
  registry-server surface uses raw SQL paginations, JSON1
  metadata queries (json_extract), and FTS5 — replicating those
  across every backend is out of proportion to the value. A
  backend-portable registry server would be a fresh refactor.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, race tests for
internal/pipeline and cmd/mnemos green.

commits.subject: feat(store): full Postgres provider implementation (Phase 3)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): full Postgres provider implementation (Phase 3)

ADR 0001 Phase 3 — flesh out the Postgres scaffold into a real
backend. The provider now connects via pgx/v5/stdlib, bootstraps
its schema inside a per-DSN namespace, and exposes every port-typed
repository. memory:// and sqlite:// remain unchanged; postgres://
joins them as a first-class option that operators can point
MNEMOS_DB_URL at today.

What ships:

- pgx/v5/stdlib dependency (database/sql driver). go.mod / go.sum
  updated.

- internal/store/postgres/postgres.go: openProvider parses the
  DSN, opens via sql.Open("pgx", ...), bootstraps the schema
  inside a transaction (CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS + SET
  search_path TO <namespace>), then returns a Conn populated with
  all nine port-typed repositories. Bootstrap is idempotent —
  schema.sql uses CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS exclusively.

- internal/store/postgres/schema.sql: in-binary copy of
  sql/postgres/schema.sql (//go:embed). Single source of truth;
  the sql/ tree is documentation-friendly.

- Nine repository implementations covering events, claims (+
  status history + ports.TrustScorer for trust scoring),
  relationships, evidence, embeddings (bytea, byte-compatible
  with SQLite via internal/embedding.EncodeVector), users, agents,
  revoked tokens, entities (+ Merge with ON CONFLICT-driven
  link rewriting), and compilation jobs. SQL idioms translate
  one-to-one from SQLite: $N placeholders, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
  / DO UPDATE, ANY($1) for IN-list predicates, jsonb for
  metadata/scopes/scope_json, timestamptz for time fields.

- internal/store/postgres/integration_test.go: 8 round-trip tests
  gated on TEST_POSTGRES_DSN. Each test creates a fresh
  per-namespace schema (mnemos_test_<nanos>) and drops it on
  cleanup. Covers schema bootstrap, event round-trip, claim
  upsert + status history attribution, TrustScorer capability,
  user/agent FK enforcement, revoked-token purge, entity
  FindOrCreate dedup + Merge, and compilation job round-trip.

- postgres_test.go: TestStoreOpen_BadDSN replaces the old
  TestStoreOpen_ReturnsNotImplemented — the provider now opens
  for real, so we exercise the connection-failure path.

- CLAUDE.md: updated bullet to reflect "full implementation via
  pgx/v5/stdlib".

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green (8 integration tests pass
against postgres:16-alpine; SKIP cleanly without
TEST_POSTGRES_DSN), make build succeeds.

Out of scope (separate later features):
- pgvector ports.VectorSearcher capability (build-tagged add-on).
- tsvector ports.TextSearcher capability (FTS analog of SQLite's
  FTS5 paths).
- CI Postgres job (docker-compose service + workflow step).

ADR 0001 status: every Tier-1 provider from §"Provider Tiers" is
now shipped. The contract is validated end-to-end.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark postgres-provider scaffold done (e3f813f)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark postgres-provider scaffold done (e3f813f)

commits.subject: feat(store): Postgres provider scaffold (Phase 3)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): Postgres provider scaffold (Phase 3)

ADR 0001 Phase 3 — register the postgres:// / postgresql:// schemes
with the store registry, validate DSN parsing including the
namespace contract, and ship the schema SQL ready for the repository
implementations to land.

What ships now:

- internal/store/postgres/postgres.go: provider registration and
  DSN parser. namespaceRE enforces ADR 0001 §3's identifier
  contract (^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,62}$). LibpqDSN strips the
  ?namespace= param so the eventual database/sql driver sees a
  clean DSN. Open returns ErrNotImplemented until repository
  implementations land — wrapped with the resolved DSN and
  namespace so operators see a clear, actionable error rather
  than "unknown provider".

- sql/postgres/schema.sql: full schema mirrored from
  sql/sqlite/schema.sql, ported to Postgres types (text PK,
  timestamptz, jsonb metadata, bytea embeddings, bigserial for
  status_history). Comments call out where pgvector and tsvector
  capabilities will plug in once the corresponding ports are
  implemented.

- 6 unit tests: namespace defaulting, query-string stripping,
  alias scheme acceptance, namespace validation rejection cases,
  ErrNotImplemented wrapping through store.Open, registration
  visibility through SupportedSchemes.

- cmd/mnemos/main.go: blank-imports the postgres provider so
  mnemos doctor and mnemos --help advertise the scheme. Operators
  who set MNEMOS_DB_URL=postgres://... get the actionable
  ErrNotImplemented error rather than a misleading "unknown
  provider" message.

- CLAUDE.md: documents the scaffold status alongside the existing
  sqlite + memory bullets.

What does NOT ship:

- pgx/v5 dependency (will land with the first repository
  implementation; until then the package is pure stdlib).
- Repository implementations (events, claims, evidence, trust,
  embeddings, users, agents, jobs, entities). Each will mirror
  the SQLite shape using equivalent Postgres SQL.
- Schema migration runner (Open will read sql/postgres/schema.sql
  and run it inside CREATE SCHEMA + SET search_path TO …).
- pgvector / tsvector capability layers (build-tagged additions).
- TEST_POSTGRES_DSN-gated integration tests + docker-compose CI
  job.

These are tracked in roady as future iterations on the same feature.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green, make build succeeds.

ADR 0001 status:
- Phase 1a registry + memory provider:           925b7f8 ✓
- Phase 1b MNEMOS_DB_URL:                        9be485e ✓
- Phase 1c cmd/mnemos call site migration:       62cb44f ✓
- Phase 2a port-lift Entity + Job:               e3c08dc ✓
- Phase 2b pipeline port refactor:               09640bc ✓
- Phase 2c sqlite.New* → conn.Xxx:               41a845d ✓
- Phase 3  Postgres scaffold:                    this commit ✓
- Phase 3+ Postgres repos + tests:               pending

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark call-site-cleanup done (41a845d)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark call-site-cleanup done (41a845d)

commits.subject: refactor(store): drop sqlite.New* in cmd/mnemos for conn.Xxx (Phase 2c)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
refactor(store): drop sqlite.New* in cmd/mnemos for conn.Xxx (Phase 2c)

ADR 0001 Phase 2c — replace direct sqlite.NewXxxRepository(db)
constructions across cmd/mnemos with conn.Xxx port-typed access.
After this commit, cmd/mnemos uses internal/store/sqlite only via
blank-import-for-registration in main.go and via the registry-
mediated openConn helper. The serve.go HTTP handlers keep the
sqlite import because they issue raw SQL against *sql.DB and
build their own RevokedTokenRepository there.

What ships:

- users.go, agents.go: conn.Users / conn.Agents / conn.RevokedTokens.
  Removed the named sqlite import.
- resolve.go: handleResolve passes *store.Conn into
  runContradictionResolution / runSupersession; both use
  conn.Claims for Upsert/UpsertWithReasonAs/SetValidity. Removed
  the sqlite import.
- entities.go: resolveEntity now takes ports.EntityRepository.
  All sites use conn.Entities. Removed the sqlite import.
- main.go: every callback uses conn.Events/Claims/Relationships/
  Embeddings/Entities. resolveContradictionClaimText takes
  ports.ClaimRepository. Trust scoring on the metrics command
  is gated by a ports.TrustScorer type assertion (nil when the
  backend doesn't implement it). WithTextSearch is engaged only
  when both repos satisfy ports.TextSearcher. Removed the named
  sqlite import; kept blank-import for registration.
- mcp.go: query_knowledge / process_text / resolveMCPActor go
  through conn.Events/Claims/Relationships/Embeddings/Jobs/Users.
  Removed the named sqlite import.
- admin.go: handleRecomputeTrust + post-dedupe trust pass use
  ports.TrustScorer assertion. Removed the named sqlite import
  (sqlcgen still imported for raw SQL paths).
- actor.go: resolveActor signature changed from *sql.DB to
  ports.UserRepository. All callers pass conn.Users.
- actor_test.go: tests use openTestStore(t) helper instead of
  raw sqlite.Open.

Out of scope (deliberately preserved on sqlite types):
- internal/pipeline/semantic_dedupe.go: raw SQL probes.
- cmd/mnemos/serve.go and registry.go HTTP handlers: take
  *sql.DB, do raw SQL, construct sqlite repos locally.
  Migrating these would require restructuring every handler
  signature for marginal value — they're SQLite-specific
  registry-server endpoints.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green, make build succeeds.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark pipeline-port-refactor done (09640bc)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark pipeline-port-refactor done (09640bc)

commits.subject: feat(pipeline): port-typed pipeline functions on *store.Conn (Phase 2b)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(pipeline): port-typed pipeline functions on *store.Conn (Phase 2b)

ADR 0001 Phase 2b — refactor the four pipeline orchestration
functions to take *store.Conn instead of *sql.DB so memory:// can
run end-to-end ingest/process/embed paths.

What ships:

- internal/pipeline/pipeline.go:
  - PersistArtifacts(ctx, conn, events, claims, links, rels):
    replaces the cross-table SQLite transaction with sequential
    port-typed repository calls. Cross-table atomicity is dropped
    in exchange for backend portability; per-repo writes remain
    individually atomic and idempotent (events use INSERT … ON
    CONFLICT DO NOTHING; claims, evidence, relationships are
    UPSERTs), so a partial-failure retry converges. claim
    status_history attribution is preserved by grouping claims
    by CreatedBy and calling UpsertWithReasonAs once per group.
    Trust scoring engages when conn.Claims satisfies
    ports.TrustScorer (SQLite + memory both do post-Phase 2a).
  - MaterializeEntities(ctx, conn, ...): unchanged logic, now via
    conn.Entities.
  - GenerateEmbeddings / GenerateClaimEmbeddings: same shape,
    now via conn.Embeddings.

- cmd/mnemos: every call site migrated.
  - main.go runJob's callback gains a *store.Conn parameter
    alongside *sql.DB so handlers can pick whichever they need;
    workflow.NewRunner now reads from conn.Jobs (port-typed).
  - autoingest.go, gitcontext.go, prcontext.go, watch.go, mcp.go,
    entities.go, admin.go updated. The few helpers that still
    need *sql.DB (existingSourcePaths, existingGitCommitSHAs,
    existingGitPRNumbers — they use SQLite json_extract) extract
    via conn.Raw.(*sql.DB) and gracefully fall back to an empty
    set on non-SQLite backends so the dedup-by-source-path
    optimisation degrades cleanly.
  - Watcher now holds a *store.Conn instead of *sql.DB; shutdown
    paths unchanged.

- cmd/mnemos/store_test_helpers_test.go: openTestStore(t)
  shared helper that opens a temp SQLite file through the registry
  and returns both the *sql.DB and *store.Conn for tests that
  still issue raw SQL probes. Phase 2c will continue trimming the
  *sql.DB callers.

- Tests: gitcontext_test.go, prcontext_test.go, autoingest_test.go,
  watch_test.go switched to openTestStore. Behaviour assertions
  unchanged.

Out of scope (Phase 2c): semantic_dedupe.go (still SQLite-specific
raw SQL probes), the cmd/mnemos sites that still construct
sqlite.NewClaimRepository / sqlite.NewEventRepository directly for
RecomputeTrust and other surfaces, the registry HTTP server's
direct repo construction. These are mechanical follow-ups now that
the pipeline is backend-agnostic.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, full
go test -race -count=1 ./... green, make build succeeds.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark port-lift-entity-job done (e3c08dc)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark port-lift-entity-job done (e3c08dc)

commits.subject: feat(store): lift Entity + CompilationJob into ports (Phase 2a)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): lift Entity + CompilationJob into ports (Phase 2a)

ADR 0001 Phase 2a — bring the last two repositories that were still
SQLite-only types into the ports layer, so cmd/mnemos and
internal/pipeline can stop reaching for sqlite.NewEntityRepository
and sqlite.NewCompilationJobRepository directly.

What ships:

- internal/ports/interfaces.go: new EntityRepository and
  CompilationJobRepository interfaces. EntityRepository surfaces
  FindOrCreate, LinkClaim, List/ListByType/FindByName,
  ListClaimsForEntity, ListEntitiesForClaim, Merge, Count, and
  ClaimIDsMissingEntityLinks. The dedup contract
  UNIQUE(normalized_name, type) on entities and
  UNIQUE(claim_id, entity_id, role) on claim_entities is now an
  interface-level guarantee, not a SQLite implementation detail.

- internal/store/store.go: Conn now exposes Entities and Jobs
  alongside the existing port-typed repositories.

- internal/store/sqlite/provider.go: populates Entities and Jobs.
  The existing SQLite repo types satisfy the new interfaces
  without changes.

- internal/store/memory/{entity,compilation_job}_repository.go:
  in-memory implementations. Entity dedup uses a (normalized_name,
  type) -> id map; claim_entities dedup uses a (claim_id,
  entity_id, role) tuple key. Merge runs under the single state
  mutex so the operation is observably atomic to other readers,
  matching the SQLite transaction's intent.
- internal/store/memory/records.go: storedEntity, entityKey,
  claimEntityKey, storedCompilationJob.

- Tests: 4 new memory behaviour tests (FindOrCreate dedup,
  LinkClaim idempotency, Merge link rewrites, Count, ClaimIDs
  missing links, CompilationJob round-trip + replace-on-id).
  Smoke tests in store_test.go + memory_test.go now check Entities
  and Jobs are populated.

Out of scope (later phases): refactoring pipeline.PersistArtifacts
and pipeline.MaterializeEntities to take *store.Conn instead of
*sql.DB; cmd/mnemos call sites still reach for
sqlite.NewCompilationJobRepository inside runJob and the MCP
process_text path. Postgres provider remains separate.

Validation: gofmt clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, go test -race
-count=1 ./... all packages green.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark cmd/mnemos call site migration done (62cb44f)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark cmd/mnemos call site migration done (62cb44f)

commits.subject: feat(store): migrate cmd/mnemos call sites to openDB (Phase 1c)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): migrate cmd/mnemos call sites to openDB (Phase 1c)

ADR 0001 Phase 1c — switch every production cmd/mnemos open path
from sqlite.Open(resolveDBPath()) to openDB(ctx) so MNEMOS_DB_URL is
honoured uniformly across the CLI, MCP server, registry server,
auto-ingest, federation, audit, and resolve commands. ~28 call
sites across 9 files.

Helper:

- openDB(ctx) (*sql.DB, *store.Conn, error) in cmd/mnemos/dsn.go.
  Opens via the registry, type-asserts Conn.Raw to *sql.DB, and
  returns a clear error when the resolved DSN points at a non-SQLite
  backend (entity & compilation_job repos and several raw-SQL
  paginations still need *sql.DB until the next port-lift phase).
- closeConn helper, drop-in for the deleted closeDB.

Migrations (preserve behaviour, including timeouts and error
messages):

- users.go (5), agents.go (5): user/agent/token CRUD and revoke.
- browse.go (2): list_claims, list_contradictions MCP tools.
- audit.go (1), audit_who.go (1): operator audit commands now
  record resolveDSN() in the export rather than the raw file path.
- resolve.go (1): operator contradiction/supersession resolution.
- mcp.go (10 + watcher): query_knowledge, process_text, metrics,
  ingest_git_log, ingest_git_prs, watch_file lifecycle, auto-ingest.
  Watcher state now holds *store.Conn instead of *sql.DB so shutdown
  closes through the same registry path.
- serve.go (1): registry HTTP server now logs DSN, not file path.
- registry.go (2): push/pull federation.
- main.go (runJob, 1): preserves first-run detection by file path
  (still SQLite-only) and reports DSN in the open error.

Cleanup:

- Removed cmd/mnemos/helpers.go (closeDB helper now orphaned —
  test packages have their own equivalent in internal/store/sqlite).

Out of scope (separate later phases): lifting EntityRepository and
CompilationJobRepository into ports, refactoring pipeline functions
that take *sql.DB to take *store.Conn, Postgres provider.

Validation: full go test -race ./... green; lint clean. Tests using
fixed temp paths (sqlite.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ...))) are
intentionally untouched — they don't go through resolveDBPath and
will be updated when the SQLite repo APIs themselves migrate.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark multi-backend-storage-mnemos_db_url done (9be485e)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark multi-backend-storage-mnemos_db_url done (9be485e)

commits.subject: feat(store): MNEMOS_DB_URL env var + openConn helper (Phase 1b)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): MNEMOS_DB_URL env var + openConn helper (Phase 1b)

ADR 0001 Phase 1b — introduce the DSN entry point so call sites
have a path off direct sqlite.Open without requiring a single
mass-migration commit.

What ships:

- cmd/mnemos/dsn.go: resolveDSN() returns MNEMOS_DB_URL untouched
  when set, otherwise wraps the legacy resolveDBPath() result as
  "sqlite://<path>". openConn(ctx) calls store.Open through the
  registry. Together they're a two-line drop-in for the
  sqlite.Open(resolveDBPath()) pattern.

- cmd/mnemos/main.go: blank-import internal/store/memory so the
  memory:// provider self-registers. (sqlite registers via the
  named import already in place.)

- cmd/mnemos/doctor.go: migrate the deep DB probe to openConn as
  the proof-of-life consumer. The check is renamed sqlite_open ->
  store_open since the doctor probes whatever backend the DSN
  points at; non-SQLite backends skip the SQLite-specific write
  probe (their open check above already proved reachability).

- README.md, CLAUDE.md, mnemos help: document MNEMOS_DB_URL
  precedence and the new pluggable-backends bullet.

- cmd/mnemos/dsn_test.go: 5 focused tests covering URL precedence,
  legacy DB_PATH wrapping, project .mnemos fallback, and
  end-to-end openConn against both memory:// and sqlite:// (legacy
  DB_PATH form).

Mass call-site migration (every sqlite.Open(resolveDBPath()) site
in cmd/mnemos and internal/pipeline) is deliberately out of scope
for this commit — that's mechanical and high-volume; tracked as a
separate roady feature.

commits.subject: chore(roady): mark multi-backend-storage-foundation done (925b7f8)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message: chore(roady): mark multi-backend-storage-foundation done (925b7f8)

commits.subject: feat(store): pluggable backend registry + memory provider (Phase 1a)
commits.author: Felix Geelhaar
commits.raw_message:
feat(store): pluggable backend registry + memory provider (Phase 1a)

ADR 0001 commits Mnemos to a stack-wide multi-backend storage contract:
URL-scheme dispatch, namespace as the universal isolation primitive,
and capability interfaces for optional features. Phase 1a lands the
foundation without touching call sites.

What ships:

- internal/store: driver registry. store.Open(ctx, dsn) parses the URL
  scheme and dispatches to a registered provider. Conn bundles every
  port-typed repository plus a Closer. Providers self-register from
  init() — duplicate registration panics so collisions surface at
  startup.

- internal/store/sqlite/provider.go: existing SQLite implementation
  repackaged behind the registry. Both `sqlite://` and `sqlite3://`
  schemes resolve to the same Open. Query parameters are accepted but
  ignored at this phase; namespace handling lands in a later phase.

- internal/store/memory: new in-process provider implementing every
  port-typed repository (events, claims + status history + trust,
  relationships, embeddings, users, revoked tokens, agents). Forces
  port purity, mirrors SQLite's idempotent-append and FK-like
  semantics where they matter, and unblocks fast hermetic tests +
  embedded use from Nous.

- internal/ports/interfaces.go: widen EventRepository and
  ClaimRepository to the union of methods callers across cmd/mnemos
  and internal/pipeline already reach for (ListAll, ListByRunID,
  UpsertWithReason, UpsertEvidence, SetValidity). Trust scoring stays
  optional via the new TrustScorer capability interface — backends
  that don't track trust remain valid ClaimRepositories.

- engine_test.go: collapse fakeClaimRepo wrappers onto embedding so
  future port additions only require touching overrides.

Subsequent phases (separate features in roady): migrate cmd/mnemos
and internal/pipeline call sites onto store.Open, add MNEMOS_DB_URL,
add the Postgres provider with pgvector/tsvector and namespace
isolation per the ADR's Tier 1 plan.

Tests: 7 store-level tests (registry + memory smoke), 12 memory
behaviour tests (round-trips, status history, TrustScorer capability,
isolation, validation). Full -race suite green; lint clean.
